steps.text: Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements, and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, 'Notice to Employees,' is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10 CFR 50.59, the license and Technical Specification (TS) requirements.
Determine if the licensee addressed radiologically significant events in accordance with regulatory and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Determine if an ALARA program has been implemented as required.
Determine if the licensee has developed, documented and implemented a radiation protection program in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Determine if the respiratory protection program meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if planned special exposures were made in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the dose to the embryo/fetus for declared pregnant women met regulatory requirements.
Inform appropriate management to determine the course of action.

preconditions.text: The inspection is for radiological controls at Class I Research and Test reactors (not for NRC material licenses or Agreement State licenses).
Inspection requirements are not already fulfilled in other inspection procedures.
There are concerns relative to other license activities or conditions.

constraints.text: If practicable, use direct observation to verify inspection requirements. Otherwise use some combination of records review, independent measurements, or discussions or walk-throughs with involved licensee personnel.

steps.text: Determine if a potential or actual hazard exists.
Consult with responsible management and the NRR project manager.
Initiate appropriate regulatory action.
Review the changes to the facility's radiation protection procedures.

preconditions.text: Before any regulatory action is taken, the inspector is to consult with responsible management and the NRR project manager.

steps.text: Ask the licensee where Form NRC-3 forms are posted.
Directly observe the Form NRC-3 postings in one or two areas.
Observe the controls for two radiologically controlled areas.

preconditions.text: Form NRC-3 postings are not readily apparent.

constraints.text: Form NRC-3 should be conspicuously posted in sufficient quantities and locations to permit workers to observe them.
Radiologically controlled areas are required to be conspicuously posted.
High radiation areas are required to be strictly controlled to prevent unauthorized or inadvertent access.
Areas occupied by operators or experimenters for long periods of time and common use areas must be controlled in accordance with licensee procedures and the ALARA program.

steps.text: Evaluate the licensee’s use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors, such as pneumatic irradiation sample transfer systems for "rabbits," beam ports, and other potential radiation work areas for experimental programs.
Evaluate the licensee’s use of shielding and controls used to remove or modify shielding.

constraints.text: The inspector is cautioned not to enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the high radiation area and the health physics precautions to be taken.

steps.text: Observe the protective clothing worn by two facility experimenters or other respective personnel during their work activities.
Conduct discussions with two individuals to determine their use of protective clothing.
Verify that personnel are instructed in the proper use and function of protective clothing in accordance with 10 CFR 19.12.
Verify that the licensee has provisions for the correct disposition of contaminated protective clothing.
Observe the personnel dosimetry worn by four facility workers, including one operator and one experimenter.
Verify that the dosimetry devices issued to facility personnel are appropriate for the type and energy of the emitted radiation and the radiation fields anticipated.
Verify that facility personnel required to use dosimeters are instructed in the proper use and function of the dosimeters.

preconditions.text: Observation of protective clothing is possible.
Observation of protective clothing is not practical.
Protective clothing is required.
Personnel are required to use dosimeters.

constraints.text: Protective clothing requirements may be found in licensee's procedures or posted precautions, and may include gloves, booties, rubber boots, hair covers, lab coats, coveralls, and rubber or plastic aprons.
Disposition of contaminated clothing may include disposal as radioactive waste, washing/decontamination, or holding for radioactive decay.
Licensee personnel dosimetry programs normally include provisions for monitoring beta, gamma, and fast and slow neutrons.

steps.text: Check alarming dosimeters (chirpers) to assure they are operational.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples, and activated or contaminated experimental equipment.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the applicable controlled area to determine if the dosimeter readings are actually recorded as required.
Review the process of processing own film or TLD badges.

preconditions.text: If alarming dosimeters (chirpers) are used
If licensee procedures require that personnel exposures on direct reading dosimeters be recorded
If the licensee processes its own film or TLD badges

constraints.text: Dosimetry must be worn as required.
Dosimetry must meet the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501 for the National Voluntary Laboratory Accreditation Program accreditation.

steps.text: Observe four facility workers leaving a controlled area.
Perform an appropriate check of all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination before leaving the controlled area.
Ensure the exit monitor is shielded from extraneous radiation sources.
Monitor hands before the probe is removed from its mounting.
Detect alpha contamination using a hand held gas flow proportional counter with a thin mylar window or by smear survey and analyses using a 2 or 4 pi gas flow proportional counter.
Perform nasal smears.

preconditions.text: Where hand held friskers are used
If alpha contamination is suspected
Where personnel contamination by alpha emitters is suspected

constraints.text: An appropriate check of all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination is required before leaving a controlled area in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart F.
The exit monitor must be shielded from extraneous radiation sources that would mask contamination.
Prevent contamination of the probe.

steps.text: Evaluate the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers, and where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe respective activities and discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals to confirm that appropriate training was actually received and understood.
Verify whether instruction appropriate to the tasks to be performed was provided to these individuals and whether there was adequate supervision of their activities.
Observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities, other than exit surveys.
Accompany a person qualified in the licensee's radiation control techniques on normal rounds, or request to be shown the manner in which such tasks are normally accomplished.
Determine whether personnel conducting these activities are qualified by training and experience through direct observation and discussion.
Perform operability checks (e.g., source and battery checks) on all instrumentation before use.
Verify whether technicians take smears or instrument readings, as appropriate, in areas that are readily accessible to facility personnel.
Examine the licensee's training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Verify whether surveys were performed as required by the licensee's procedures.
Verify whether the surveys indicate that exposure rates in these areas are consistent with the licensee's ALARA program.

preconditions.text: Concerns arise regarding whether technicians are sufficiently trained.

constraints.text: All instrumentation used during these activities are required to be operable, calibrated, and maintained as required by 10 CFR 20.1501(b) and the licensee's procedures.
Particular attention is to be given to beam ports, experimental stations, and ion exchangers for cleanup of primary water, where applicable.

steps.text: Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
Verify if the licensee evaluated personnel occupancy of these areas and the potential dose to occupants.
Check for excessive levels of contamination within restricted areas and at the exits from the restricted area.
Perform independent radiological surveys as needed to verify licensee assumptions or measurements.
Compare inspector measurements with those completed by the licensee in the same areas to verify consistency.
Review surveys conducted by the licensee during both shutdown and operations following changes in shielding.
Verify there is reasonable agreement with the licensee's records.

preconditions.text: Radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area.
Radiation levels above 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D are detected outside the restricted area.
Surveys are performed by an inspector.

constraints.text: Highly radioactive experiments or objects are adjacent to a wall, and are unshielded.
Good housekeeping practices suggest a barrier any time that contamination levels exceed double the background unless the contamination is removable and the area is immediately decontaminated.

steps.text: Determine how the licensee assures that airborne contamination levels are within the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR Part 20, Subparts C and D.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee’s radiation surveys.
Verify if all survey, sampling, and monitoring instruments have current calibrations appropriate for the types and energies of radiation to be detected.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures.

preconditions.text: The potential exists for airborne contamination
The facility performs their own calibrations

constraints.text: Information Notice 85-92, 'Surveys of Wastes Before Disposal from Nuclear Reactor Facilities,' is to be used as technical guidance.
Additional guidance can be found in 10 CFR 20.1902.
ANSI N323, 'Radiation Protection Instrumentation Test and Calibration,' 1978, provides additional guidance in this area.

steps.text: Review 50 percent of the facility's personnel dosimetry records.
Review the issue further to determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee's ALARA program.

Determine if the licensee reported the over exposures in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.2202 and 20.2203, how the licensee addressed the problem, and inform appropriate management and the responsible NRR project manager.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.
Review any organizational change involving the radiation protection manager, giving particular attention to the reporting chain above the facility director if required by the TS.

preconditions.text: If individuals routinely exceed 20 percent of the exposure limits
If there were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits

constraints.text: The licensee is required to keep exposure records (e.g., TLD or film badge records) on form NRC-5 or equivalent (See 10 CFR 20.2106).
Be sensitive to changes that reduce the ability of the radiation protection.

steps.text: Verify if personnel who oversee the radiation protection program, and those with radiation protection duties, are qualified by training and experience to perform intended functions.
Review major radiation control events at the facility since the last inspection.
Verify if major incidents were reported to the NRC in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2202.
Verify if corrective actions were reviewed by the safety review committee and were consistent with facility TS and procedures for the resolution of the problems.
Inform appropriate management and the NRR project manager.

preconditions.text: The requirement to review major radiation control events has not already been adequately addressed during the assessment of personnel exposure records, in the inspector's professional judgement.
Major incidents occurred.
Radiologically significant events were not effectively addressed by the licensee.

constraints.text: Events having radiologically related significance are required to be addressed in accordance with the facility TS, procedures and administrative controls.

steps.text: Verify ALARA implementation for one routinely performed experiment or process having an actual or potential for a relatively high radiological exposure.
Discuss ALARA considerations with operations or radiation protection personnel to determine the effectiveness of the licensee's ALARA program.
Verify if facility supervisors periodically perform observations of staff effectiveness in radiological work practices, work monitoring, procedural compliance, and survey adequacy.
Verify if the radiation protection program is periodically reviewed and audits are performed by the radiation safety group.

preconditions.text: The ALARA inspection has not been covered and adequately evaluated under other inspection requirements.

steps.text: Audit and evaluate the radiation protection program to assure compliance with 10 CFR Part 20, the TSs, and other program requirements.
Limit intakes of radioactive material consistent with ALARA.

constraints.text: Use respiratory protection equipment (and other controls) to limit intakes of radioactive material.
